title: SSC CGL vs SSC CHSL 2026 — Which SSC Exam Should You Choose?
meta_description: SSC CGL vs CHSL 2026 — salary comparison, posts, eligibility, difficulty, and which exam suits graduates vs 12th pass candidates.

hero.one_liner: CGL gives ₹44K+ posts (Tax Inspector, Auditor) while CHSL gives ₹25K+ posts (LDC, DEO) — same agency, dramatically different career outcomes and lifetime earnings.

sections.heading: SSC CGL vs CHSL: Complete Comparison
rows: Tax Inspector, Auditor, SI CBI, Inspector (CBDT/CBIC)
LDC, Junior Secretariat Asst, DEO, Postal Asst

sections.heading: Salary and Career Growth: The Lifetime Earnings Difference
content: Starting Salary Comparison (Age 25, First Day)
SSC CGL starting at Tax Inspector: Basic ₹44,900 (Level 7), with DA (7.5%) and HRA (20%) in a metro city = ₹44,900 + ₹3,400 (DA) + ₹8,980 (HRA) = ₹57,280/month. Total in-hand: ~₹65,000-70,000/month.
SSC CHSL starting at LDC: Basic ₹25,500 (Level 4), with DA and HRA (metro) = ₹25,500 + ₹1,900 (DA) + ₹5,100 (HRA) = ₹32,500/month. Total in-hand: ~₹35,000-40,000/month.
SALARY DIFFERENCE AT START: ₹25,000-30,000/month (CHSL person earns 40-45% less from day 1).
Career Progression: 10 Years In
SSC CGL Tax Inspector → promoted to Senior Tax Inspector (Level 8) in 5-7 years → ₹60,000/month basic. Then promoted to Superintendent (Level 9) in 10-12 years → ₹85,000/month basic.
By age 35: earning ₹1,00,000+/month. SSC CHSL LDC → promoted to UDC (Upper Division Clerk, Level 5) in 6-10 years → ₹32,000/month basic.
Further promotion to Section Officer (Level 6) happens rarely or very late (15+ years). By age 35: earning ₹40,000-50,000/month.
CAREER GROWTH DIFFERENCE: CGL officer grows 100-150%, CHSL officer grows 40-50%.
Lifetime Earnings Calculation (30-Year Career)
SSC CGL: Starts at ₹65K/month → average growth → ₹1,20,000/month by age 55. Over 30 years: approximately ₹2.4 crore total earnings.
SSC CHSL: Starts at ₹35K/month → slower growth → ₹55,000/month by age 55. Over 30 years: approximately ₹1.15 crore total earnings.
LIFETIME DIFFERENCE: CGL person earns ₹1.25 crore more than CHSL person. This is life-changing money.

sections.heading: Exam Difficulty and Preparation Strategy
content: CGL Tier 2 Exam (The Differentiator)
CGL Tier 2 is the hardest part. Candidates must solve Math at graduation-level (algebra, geometry, trigonometry).
The math section is deliberately hard to separate wheat from chaff. Typical difficulty: 10-15% candidates clear Tier 1, and 5-10% of those clear Tier 2.
Tier 2 is the true test of intellect.
CHSL Tier 2 Exam
CHSL Tier 2 is also math + English, but math is at 12th-standard level. Candidates who mastered 12th math can handle this easily.
The difficulty barrier is much lower. ~25-30% clear Tier 1, and 15-20% of those clear Tier 2. CHSL is more accessible.
Preparation Strategy
If you're a graduate confident in maths: Prepare for CGL. The effort is 20-30% more than CHSL, but the payoff is enormous.

If you're 12th pass or weak in advanced maths: CHSL is realistic. If you're currently studying graduation (in first/second year): Learn CGL math alongside your college coursework — this gives you advantage when you appear for CGL after graduation.

sections.heading: Which Should You Choose: The Decision Matrix
rows: I'm in college (2nd/3rd year) and good at maths
Extra 2-3 years of preparation time. CGL's higher salary makes extra effort worth it.
I'm a fresh graduate, good in all subjects
You meet eligibility right now. Age 22-25 is ideal for CGL preparation. ₹25,000/month salary difference is massive.
I'm 12th pass and need government job urgently
CGL requires graduation. Don't wait 4 years for college. Join CHSL, work, earn, and grow.
I'm 12th pass, can do graduation via distance mode
Get a government job via CHSL (within 1-2 years). While working as CHSL, do graduation through correspondence. Then appear for CGL at age 25-28. Path: job security + upgrade.
I'm 28+ years old and graduation complete
CGL if age < 32; CHSL if uncertain
If under 32, CGL age limit still allows you. If 32+, CHSL is your only option (age limit 27 for fresh, but some states have 30+).
I'm not sure which exam is easier
Study CGL, appear for CHSL too
CGL prep covers CHSL 90%. Both exams conducted same year, different dates. Appear for both — CHSL is backup.
CHSL ensures government job by age 25-26. Then attempt CGL. Never left without a government job.

sections.heading: The Hybrid Smart Strategy
content: Best Approach for Most Students
If you're a college student: Prepare for CGL while finishing your degree. Simultaneously appear for CHSL.

CHSL is easier and acts as a backup. Success path: Clear CHSL in your 3rd/4th year of college → Start working as CHSL LDC → Continue CGL preparation in evenings → Clear CGL 1-2 years later → Resign CHSL, join CGL post.
You never have unemployment, and you upgrade your career while employed.
Why Simultaneously Is Smart
CGL and CHSL are different exams with different dates. Preparing for CGL automatically makes CHSL easy (it's a subset).
Appearing for both in the same year is just ₹1,500-2,000 extra fee and same effort. If you clear CHSL (easier), you have a government job in hand.
While employed, CGL becomes much easier to prepare for (no pressure, income security). Many successful CGL officers cleared CHSL first, then upgraded.
Worst Approach
Waiting till after graduation to start preparation, hoping to only prepare for CGL. This often backfires: overconfidence, irregular preparation, one exam, no backup.
Better to start from college, attempt both, secure CHSL, then upgrade to CGL.

sections.heading: Real Post Analysis — Is Tax Inspector Better Than LDC?
content: Tax Inspector (CGL Post)
Roles: Audit company tax returns, investigate fraud, process tax payments. Work involves: Desk work + field visits.
Difficulty: Moderate — requires understanding of tax law but mostly follows procedure. Career: Promotions are merit-based and regular.
Growth to senior management level. Stress: Moderate (politicians/businessmen can pressure, but protected by rules).
Job satisfaction: High if you value public service and following rules.
LDC (CHSL Post)
Roles: Maintain office records, type documents, assist senior officers. Work involves: Mostly desk-based, routine clerical work.
Difficulty: Low — straightforward tasks. Career: Promotions are slow and seniority-based.
Ceiling is junior management level. Stress: Low (no policy-making pressure).
Job satisfaction: Lower (repetitive work) but secure and stress-free.
The Choice
Tax Inspector: Higher salary, more challenging work, faster promotion, requires more ability. Better for ambitious people.
LDC: Lower salary, routine work, slower promotion, requires less ability. Better for people who prioritize security over growth.
There's nothing wrong with either choice — it depends on your ambition level.

faqs.question: I'm 12th pass. Can I appear for CGL later, after graduation?
faqs.answer: Yes! CGL eligibility is 'graduation' with no minimum age (except max 32). You can complete graduation at any age (even correspondence mode while working as CHSL LDC), then appear for CGL till age 32. Many CHSL employees become CGL officers this way. Strategy: Get CHSL job now, do graduation at night, appear for CGL at 26-28. This is very doable.

faqs.question: Can I appear for both CGL and CHSL in the same year?
faqs.answer: Yes! CHSL is usually conducted January-February. CGL is usually June-July. You can appear for both in the same year. In fact, this is RECOMMENDED. Both have 80%+ preparation overlap. Apply to both, prepare once, sit both exams. If you clear CHSL, you have a backup. If you don't clear either, you still have other chances.

faqs.question: I'm 25, graduated 2 years ago. Which exam should I choose?
faqs.answer: CGL! You're 25 with 7 years left before age limit (32). You're young enough to handle CGL's tougher exam. The salary difference (₹25,000+/month) justifies 3-6 months extra preparation. Prepare for 6-8 months and appear. Even if you don't clear first attempt, you have 5-6 more attempts before turning 32.

faqs.question: CGL Tier 2 Math is too hard for me. Should I give up and do CHSL instead?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily. Many people say CGL math is hard but actually pass it when they study properly. Your first Tier 2 attempt is not the end. You get 6 attempts till age 32. Use your first 2 attempts for learning, then write seriously. Alternatively, do CHSL as backup while preparing harder for CGL. Don't give up prematurely.

faqs.question: I cleared CHSL. Should I still attempt CGL?
faqs.answer: Absolutely YES. You now have a government job (secure), income (no pressure to clear CGL), and employment (easier to prepare while employed). Attempt CGL for the next 5-7 years. Clearing CGL will give you ₹20,000-30,000/month additional salary. Even if you never clear CGL, you're already secured with CHSL. It's pure upside.

faqs.question: What if I don't clear CGL before age 32 but have CHSL?
faqs.answer: You continue as CHSL officer. There's no shame — it's a government job with security, pension, and decent salary. Many CHSL officers never clear CGL and have successful, satisfying careers. The plan was always: CHSL ensures security, CGL is the upgrade (if it happens). Not upgrading is not failure.

disclaimer: Post names, salary levels, and vacancy numbers are approximate and change annually with government revisions. Verify with ssc.gov.in for current recruitment notification details.
